data.title: Santander Flags Strong First Quarter, Sticks to Targets
data.description:
By Elena Vardon 




  Banco Santander said it has had a strong start to the year, with growth in customers and revenue, and backed its targets despite increasing global economic uncertainty. 



  The Spanish bank has seen a continuation of the positive trends seen in previous years over the first quarter, with performance supporting its full-year and midterm targets, Chair Ana Botin said ahead of its annual general meeting Friday. 



  "The world now faces the scenario of higher inflation and lower growth, threats that become more likely with each passing day. Their severity will depend on the duration of the conflict [in the Persian Gulf] and its impact on global energy supply," Botin said. Santander's geographic diversification mitigates risk by reducing volatility, she said. 



  At the meeting, shareholders are set to vote to approve a dividend of 12.5 European cents (14.4 U.S. cents) a share on last year's earnings as well as a capital increase for its acquisition of Webster in the U.S. 





  Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 27, 2026 03:45 ET (07:45 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Collagen Peptide Market 2026-2036 | Europe & US Demand Surge with Top Companies GELITA, Darling Ingredients
data.description:
Collagen Peptide Market: Precision Hydrolysis, Clinical Validation, and Functional Nutrition Drive Growth

NEWARK, DE / ACCESS Newswire / March 27, 2026 / The global Collagen Peptide Market is experiencing strong momentum as demand for clinically validated, bioavailable protein ingredients accelerates across nutraceuticals, functional beverages, and medical nutrition. Valued at USD 2.8 billion in 2026, the market is projected to reach USD 7.0 billion by 2036, expanding at a CAGR of 9.7% during the forecast period.

According to Future Market Insights (FMI), the market is transitioning from commodity protein supply to precision-engineered bioactive peptides, where molecular weight consistency and clinical substantiation determine commercial value.

Collagen Peptide Market Metrics (2026-2036)

Market Indicator

Value

Market Value (2036)

USD 7.0 Billion

9.7%

Market Transformation: From Generic Protein to Targeted Bioactive Peptides

The collagen peptide market is undergoing a structural evolution, where formulators are shifting away from general protein supplementation toward targeted physiological outcomes, including:

  • Skin health and anti-aging

  • Joint and bone support

  • Sports recovery and muscle repair

This transition is driven by the need for:

  • Clinical substantiation of claims

  • Standardized molecular weight profiles

  • Verified bioavailability in human systems

Brands that fail to adopt precision-cleaved peptides risk losing shelf space in premium retail and pharmacy channels.

Technology Focus: Enzymatic Hydrolysis Defines Competitive Advantage

At the core of the market lies enzymatic hydrolysis, a process that determines:

  • Peptide molecular weight

  • Solubility in liquids

  • Absorption efficiency

Precision in hydrolysis directly impacts:

  • Clinical efficacy

  • Product stability in beverages

  • Regulatory approval for health claims

Suppliers capable of delivering consistent, batch-to-batch molecular profiles command premium pricing and long-term supply contracts.

Segment Insights: Bovine Sources and Type I Peptides Lead

By Source Type: Bovine Collagen (54.3% Share)

Bovine collagen dominates due to:

  • Large-scale raw material availability

  • Established processing infrastructure

  • Cost efficiency for mass-market applications

Its strong supply base enables manufacturers to maintain stable pricing and large-volume production.

By Product Type: Type I Collagen (43.7% Share)

Type I collagen leads due to its:

  • Direct relevance to skin and connective tissue health

  • Strong clinical validation for beauty and wellness applications

  • High solubility and compatibility with functional beverages

This segment is critical for brands targeting premium nutraceutical and cosmetic formulations.

Demand Drivers: Functional Nutrition and Beverage Innovation

Nutraceutical Growth

Collagen peptides are widely used in:

  • Dietary supplements

  • Sports nutrition products

  • Clinical nutrition formulations

Growing consumer awareness of preventive healthcare and beauty-from-within concepts is driving adoption.

Functional Beverages

The rise of ready-to-drink collagen beverages is creating demand for:

  • Highly soluble peptides

  • Temperature-stable formulations

  • Clear, non-gelling protein systems

Formulators must ensure that collagen peptides remain stable in acidic and cold-fill environments, making solubility a key selection criterion.

Regional Outlook: India and Europe Drive Growth Dynamics

India (9.2% CAGR)

India leads global growth, driven by:

  • Rapid formalization of the nutraceutical sector

  • Increasing regulatory enforcement

  • Rising consumer demand for functional health products

United Kingdom (9.1% CAGR)

The UK market is shaped by:

  • Strong emphasis on supply chain transparency

  • Retailer-driven sustainability mandates

  • Demand for traceable collagen sourcing

Germany (7.7% CAGR)

Germany focuses on:

  • High-purity, clinically validated ingredients

  • Pharmaceutical-grade collagen peptides

  • Advanced formulation infrastructure

United States (7.3% CAGR)

The U.S. market benefits from:

  • Strong integration between meat processing and collagen extraction

  • Mature dietary supplement industry

  • Demand for consistent, high-quality inputs

Japan (6.9% CAGR)

Japan emphasizes:

  • Ultra-refined marine collagen peptides

  • Aging population-driven demand

  • Advanced formulation precision

Market Dynamics: Standardization and Clinical Proof Drive Growth

Key Drivers

  • Increasing demand for clinically proven nutraceutical ingredients

  • Growth in functional beverages and ready-to-drink products

  • Rising focus on beauty and wellness applications

Key Challenges

  • Variability in enzymatic hydrolysis processes

  • High capital investment required for advanced processing

  • Need for stringent quality control and batch consistency

Key Opportunities

  • Development of bioactive peptide profiling technologies

  • Expansion of traceable and sustainable sourcing models

  • Integration into medical nutrition and recovery formulations

Competitive Landscape: Branded Ingredients and Traceability Lead

The collagen peptide market is led by established manufacturers with strong expertise in hydrolysis and formulation support.

Key Players Include:

  • GELITA

  • Nitta Gelatin

  • Weishardt

  • Italgel

  • Juncà Gelatines

  • Ewald-Gelatine

  • Gelco

  • CollaSwiss

These companies compete based on:

  • Branded collagen peptide platforms

  • Clinical validation and documentation

  • Supply chain traceability

  • Consistent product performance

Outlook: Precision and Proof Will Define Market Leadership

Looking ahead to 2036, the collagen peptide market will be defined by:

  • Standardized molecular weight specifications

  • Clinical biomarker validation for absorption

  • Integration into targeted health formulations

As the industry moves toward evidence-based functional nutrition, suppliers that invest in precision hydrolysis, clinical research, and traceable sourcing will dominate, while commodity gelatin producers face increasing competitive pressure.

data.title: Global High-Stress PCR HDPE Detergent Bottles Market to Reach USD 14.7 Billion by 2036 Led by India, Berry Global, ALPLA
data.description:
Rapid growth in PCR HDPE detergent bottles is driven by plastic regulations, retailer sustainability mandates, and the shift toward recycled packaging supply chains

NEWARK, DE / ACCESS Newswire / March 27, 2026 / The global high-stress PCR HDPE detergent bottles market is entering a decisive transformation phase as sustainability mandates and recycled content regulations reshape rigid packaging economics worldwide. Valued at USD 3.6 billion in 2026, the market is projected to reach USD 14.7 billion by 2036, expanding at a robust CAGR of 15.1%.

This rapid expansion is being driven by extended producer responsibility regulations, plastic tax frameworks, and retailer sustainability scorecards that are forcing detergent and home care brands to transition toward recycled packaging at scale.

According to a comprehensive strategic outlook from Future Market Insights (FMI), the transition toward high-stress PCR HDPE bottles is not merely a sustainability initiative but a structural shift in packaging engineering, procurement strategy, and supply chain economics. Companies that delay qualification of recycled HDPE packaging risk regulatory penalties, retailer delisting, and long-term cost disadvantages as recycled resin supply becomes increasingly contracted.

Market Metrics Snapshot (2026-2036)

Market Metric

Value

Market Value (2026)

USD 3.6 Billion

Forecast Value (2036)

USD 14.7 Billion

CAGR

15.1%

Leading Bottle Format

Handleware

Leading PCR Content Tier

30-50% PCR

Leading Application

Laundry Care

Fastest Growing Market

India

Unlock Growth Potential - Request Your Sample Now and Explore Market Opportunities!
https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-32470

The Engineering Shift: From Virgin Polymer to Recycled Structural Packaging

The rigid detergent bottle industry is undergoing a technical transformation as packaging engineers redesign containers to accommodate the weaker melt strength and structural variability of post-consumer recycled resins. Unlike virgin polymer bottles, high-stress PCR HDPE containers must withstand environmental stress cracking, heavy pallet loads, chemical exposure from concentrated detergents, and long shelf-life storage conditions.

Production economics are also shifting. Blow molding operators must install advanced melt filtration systems, blending silos, and multi-layer co-extrusion equipment to process inconsistent recycled feedstock. This capital-intensive transition is consolidating manufacturing power among specialized converters capable of managing recycled resin variability.

Industry analysts describe this transition as a shift toward "technical packaging qualification economics," where supplier selection depends on drop-test survival rates, stress crack resistance, and long-term chemical compatibility rather than unit price alone.

Regulation and Procurement Pressure Reshaping Packaging Strategy

Procurement directors at FMCG and home care companies are facing mounting regulatory pressure to integrate recycled content into packaging portfolios. Plastic tax frameworks across Europe and North America are penalizing companies that fail to meet minimum recycled content thresholds, fundamentally altering the cost structure of virgin HDPE packaging.

Retailers are also introducing sustainability scorecards that evaluate packaging recyclability and recycled content inclusion. Brands that fail to comply risk losing shelf space in major retail chains, accelerating the transition toward PCR packaging solutions.

At the same time, packaging engineers must balance recycled content inclusion against environmental stress cracking resistance failure rates, particularly for bottles storing chemically aggressive laundry detergents and cleaning products.

E-Commerce, Logistics, and Structural Packaging Requirements

The growth of e-commerce distribution has increased packaging performance requirements for detergent bottles. Products now travel longer distances through complex logistics networks, increasing the importance of drop-test performance, seam integrity, and pallet stacking resistance.

Warehouse pallet failures caused by stress cracking in recycled HDPE bottles represent significant financial risk, leading packaging engineers to redesign bottle base geometries, wall thickness distribution, and handle structures to accommodate recycled material properties.

As a result, packaging design for PCR HDPE detergent bottles is becoming a highly specialized engineering discipline combining polymer science, logistics performance testing, and regulatory compliance.

Segment Spotlight: Handleware, PCR Content, and Laundry Applications

Several key segments are shaping the structure of the high-stress PCR HDPE detergent bottles market:

  • Handleware Bottles: Handleware formats account for 39% of the market share in 2026, driven by the ergonomic necessity of safely handling heavy liquid containers made from recycled polymers with lower structural strength.

  • 30-50% PCR Content: This segment holds 44% share in 2026, representing the optimal balance between sustainability targets and mechanical performance. Higher recycled content often requires expensive compatibilizers and multi-layer extrusion technology.

  • Laundry Care Applications: Laundry detergent packaging dominates the market with 49% share, reflecting the massive global volume of liquid surfactants transported through retail supply chains.

  • 1-2 Liter Bottles: This capacity segment holds 37% share, representing the optimal balance between consumer purchasing behavior and safe structural performance for recycled containers.

  • Converter-Led Production: Converter-led manufacturing accounts for 58% share, as specialized recycling and extrusion infrastructure requires significant capital investment beyond the capabilities of most captive bottle manufacturing operations.

Regional Powerhouses: Asia Leads Adoption

Regional adoption of high-stress PCR HDPE detergent bottles varies significantly based on recycling infrastructure maturity and regulatory enforcement.

  • India (17.2% CAGR): India is emerging as the fastest-growing market due to aggressive plastic waste management regulations and rapid growth in the liquid detergent sector. Domestic recycling infrastructure expansion is supporting recycled packaging adoption across FMCG industries.

  • China (16.4% CAGR): China continues to lead in chemical recycling infrastructure and recycled resin production, enabling consistent feedstock supply for high-performance PCR HDPE packaging.

  • Brazil (15.8% CAGR): Brazil's growth is driven by localized supply chains shifting away from virgin plastics and increasing investment in recycling infrastructure.

  • United States (14.1% CAGR): Growth is driven by retailer sustainability mandates and plastic tax frameworks encouraging recycled packaging adoption.

  • Germany (13.4% CAGR) and United Kingdom (13.1% CAGR): European markets are driven by strict regulatory mandates and retailer sustainability scorecards requiring recycled content packaging.

  • Japan (12.5% CAGR): Japan benefits from highly efficient municipal recycling systems producing high-quality recycled HDPE feedstock.

Dynamics of the Decade: Circular Packaging and Recycling Infrastructure

Looking toward 2036, several major trends are expected to reshape the competitive landscape for recycled HDPE detergent bottles:

  • Bottle-to-Bottle Recycling Systems: Closed-loop recycling systems will become critical for securing consistent recycled resin feedstock and reducing dependence on volatile spot markets.

  • Chemical Recycling Expansion: Advanced chemical recycling facilities will enable converters to process heavily contaminated plastic waste into high-quality recycled HDPE suitable for detergent packaging.

  • Multi-Layer Co-Extrusion Packaging: Multi-layer bottle structures will become standard, allowing recycled material to be used in the bottle core while virgin resin protects the outer and inner layers.

  • Machine Vision Quality Sorting: Automated optical inspection systems will improve recycled resin consistency and reduce bottle failure rates during logistics and storage.

Competitive Landscape: Converter Capabilities Define Market Leadership

The high-stress PCR HDPE detergent bottles market is highly technical, and specialized converters are gaining significant pricing power due to the complexity of processing recycled materials.

Leading companies are investing heavily in melt filtration systems, odor control technology, and multi-layer extrusion infrastructure to secure long-term supply contracts with FMCG companies.

Key Players in the High-Stress PCR HDPE Detergent Bottles Market include:

  • Berry Global Group, Inc.

  • ALPLA Group

  • Graham Packaging Company

  • Silgan Plastics

  • Plastipak Holdings, Inc.

  • Logoplaste

  • CKS Packaging, Inc.

These companies compete primarily on technical capability, drop-test performance, recycled content inclusion capability, and long-term supply reliability rather than price alone.

Strategic Outlook: Packaging Becomes a Supply Chain Strategy

The high-stress PCR HDPE detergent bottles market is evolving from a packaging procurement decision into a long-term supply chain strategy. Companies that secure recycled resin supply contracts, invest in packaging engineering redesign, and qualify recycled bottle formats early will gain a significant competitive advantage.

By 2036, recycled HDPE detergent bottles are expected to become the industry standard rather than a sustainability niche, fundamentally transforming rigid packaging procurement strategies across the global home care sector.

data.title: AB InBev's Board Chairman Martin Barrington to Retire
data.description:
By Aimee Look 




  Anheuser-Busch InBev said its chairman of the board, Martin Barrington, would retire from his position, after serving in the role for seven years. 



  The Belgian group, which houses Michelob, Corona and Stella Artois, said Barrington will retire from his role on the board after the Annual Shareholders' Meeting on April 29. 



  Barrington has been on the board for more than nine years, and his retirement follows the company's Corporate Governance Charter provision respecting retirement at age 70, it said. 



  For the restricted share director seat vacated by Barrington's retirement, restricted shareholders proposed William Gifford, Jr for the position. Gifford previously was an AB InBev director and is the retiring CEO of Altria Group, the company said. His appointment is subject to shareholder approval at the Annual Shareholders' Meeting. 



  The directors will give input for a new chairman, who will be appointed after the meeting. 





  Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 27, 2026 02:45 ET (06:45 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Asian Equities Mixed as Markets Watch Middle East — Update
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui and Sherry Qin 




  Asian equity markets came off their lows for the day be mixed in Friday afternoon's trade as investors weighed developments in the Middle East. 



  Japan's Nikkei Stock Average was 0.2% lower, South Korea's Kospi was off 0.5%, and Australia's S&P/ASX 200 benchmark index was 0.1% lower. Among the gainers, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.8% and China's Shanghai Composite Index put up 0.75%. 



  The Pentagon is considering sending up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East to give President Trump more military options, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing Department of Defense officials with knowledge of the planning. This force would be added to around 5,000 Marines and the thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division who have already been ordered to the Middle East. 



  However, Trump said Thursday that he had extended a pause on U.S. military strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure for 10 more days to April 6 so that negotiations can take place. The Wall Street Journal reported, citing peace talk mediators, that Iran hasn't requested the 10-day pause and has yet to submit its final response to the U.S.'s 15-point plan to end the war. 



  Doubts about de-escalation in the Middle East conflict have lingered, said two FX strategists at OCBC Group Research in a research report. "The broader growth drag from higher oil and the associated risk-off tone is now dominating," the strategists added. 



  The U.S. dollar Index was last flat at 99.86, but it has been trending higher this month and now near the psychologically-important 100 level. XS.com analyst Rania Gule thinks that signals rising global demand for safe-haven assets in an environment marked by growing geopolitical uncertainty. 



  Risk-sensitive and energy-importing currencies were mixed on Friday. The dollar edged 0.15% lower to 159.57 yen but was 0.3% higher at 60.32 Philippine peso. The dollar rose to a record against the Indian rupee on Friday afternoon, and was last up 0.3% at 94.53 rupees. 



  Precious metals have regained momentum with spot gold up 1.9% at $4,462.01 a troy ounce and spot silver rising 2.9% to $69.93 an troy ounce. 



  Meanwhile, front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures declined 0.8% to $93.72 per barrel and front-month Brent crude oil futures slipped 0.3% to $107.68 a barrel, according to ICE data. 



  "Looking into next week, oil direction will depend heavily on whether ceasefire rhetoric turns into concrete action," Phillip Nova analyst Priyanka Sachdeva said. 





  Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com and Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 27, 2026 02:33 ET (06:33 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Novartis to Buy Biotech Excellergy for Up to $2 Billion
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud 




  Novartis said it agreed to buy Excellergy for up to $2 billion, bulking up its immunology portfolio with a biotech company that specializes in treatments for food allergy and other diseases. 



  The Swiss pharmaceutical company said Friday that the deal for privately-held Excellergy will add a drug candidate in early-stage trials with potential to complement its existing allergy portfolio. 



  The deal follows Novartis's agreement last week to buy a breast-cancer drug candidate from Synnovation Therapeutics for up to $3 billion, as the company turns to dealmaking to fill its pipeline and offset patent expirations. 



  Novartis said the acquisition builds on its long-standing presence in allergic disease. 



  The lead drug in Excellergy's portfolio is a so-called anti-IgE antibody. Novartis said the drug offers a differentiated mechanism that, if confirmed clinically, could support earlier symptom relief, stronger disease control and more convenient dosing across food allergy, hives, allergic asthma and other diseases. 





  Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 27, 2026 02:36 ET (06:36 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ABIONYX Pharma Achieves Major Milestone in apoA-I Biomanufacturing with Breakthrough in Synthetic Sphingomyelin Production
data.description:
ABIONYX Pharma Achieves Major Milestone in apoA-I Biomanufacturing with Breakthrough in Synthetic Sphingomyelin Production

Regulatory News:

ABIONYX Pharma, (FR0012616852 – ABNX – eligible for PEA PME), a next-generation biopharmaceutical company developing innovative therapies in sepsis and critical care based on an exclusive apoA-I technology platform, today announced a significant technological and industrial breakthrough combining high-performance apoA-I bioproduction with exclusive mastery of ultra-pure synthetic sphingomyelin, paving the way for a new class of sepsis therapeutics.

A First-in-Class Scientific and Industrial Breakthrough

As part of its strategy to enable large-scale industrial production of CER-001, ABIONYX Pharma has developed a new manufacturing process allowing:

  • A threefold increase in sphingomyelin production yield, one of the two key components of CER-001
  • A synthetic component with exceptional purity and structural quality, meeting the most stringent pharmaceutical standards.

All synthesis processes for this component are based on fully proprietary patents, providing ABIONYX Pharma with a major technological and competitive barrier.

A Critical Component Mastered at the Highest Level

Sphingomyelin is a strategic lipid widely recognized for its structural and functional properties. Within the CER-001 biomedicine, it plays a central role:

  • It stabilizes the ApoA-I protein in its optimal functional conformation
  • It gives CER-001 biological and therapeutic properties fully comparable to natural HDL found in the human body
  • It exhibits high intrinsic stability in pure form without added stabilizers—unlike conventional sphingomyelins—strengthening manufacturing robustness and pharmaceutical-grade quality.

Sphingomyelin is also used as a key ingredient in other pharmaceutical applications, including advanced cosmetics, due to its ability to stabilize and protect biological structures. This may open additional commercial opportunities for ABIONYX Pharma.

The use of 100% synthetic sphingomyelin, replacing traditional sources (egg or bovine), eliminates:

  • Any risk of contamination
  • Variability in raw materials
  • Allergenic risks

With this achievement, the Company overcomes a major technological barrier in the control and production of CER-001.

Strategic Positioning in Sepsis: A High-Unmet-Need Market

ABIONYX Pharma is advancing its platform in sepsis, a global medical emergency characterized by severe systemic dysregulation and a substantial unmet therapeutic need.

The combination of highly purified apoA-I, proprietary synthetic sphingomyelin and optimized formulation technologies positions the company to deliver differentiated therapeutic approaches aimed at restoring essential biological functions in sepsis patients.

A Strong Benefit for the Bioproduction of the Two Validation Batches Prior to Marketing Authorization in LCAT Deficiency

This breakthrough in synthetic sphingomyelin production will also directly benefit the manufacturing of the two validation batches required ahead of Marketing Authorization (MAA) in LCAT deficiency. By ensuring high purity, reproducibility, and secured supply, this innovation significantly de-risks the production process and supports timely execution of these critical batches. These validation lots represent a key milestone for the Company, as they are essential to advancing CER-001 toward regulatory approval.

A Scalable, Best-in-Class Biomanufacturing Platform

This milestone reinforces ABIONYX Pharma’s positioning as a leader in advanced biomanufacturing within the biotech sector. The company demonstrates its ability to:

  • Manufacture complex, high-value therapeutic proteins at scale
  • Control critical lipid components at an unprecedented level of purity
  • Integrate these elements into differentiated, functional drug formulations

This achievement validates the industrial scalability of the apoA-I platform, strengthens supply chain security, and supports upcoming clinical and regulatory milestones. It also creates new opportunities for strategic partnerships with global industry players.

About ABIONYX Pharma

ABIONYX Pharma is a next-generation biopharma company pioneering a new therapeutic era in sepsis and critical care through breakthrough biotherapies that address life-threatening conditions with no effective treatments. Leveraging its proprietary apoA-I–based technology platform, ABIONYX Pharma develops innovative biologics and HDL-derived vectors that target the immune and inflammatory dysregulation underlying sepsis and other severe diseases. With a strong scientific foundation and a growing network of global clinical partners, ABIONYX Pharma aims to redefine the standard of care in sepsis and to bring critical care solutions to patients worldwide.
